title: Complete DDD application layer and port boundaries
summary: TASK-30 acceptance met and verified: application/ports/adapters split in place, Pi imports isolated, deprecated package scope blocked, core/engine folders deleted, runtime import and live codewiki_state pass.

goal.outcome: Core CodeWiki workflows are callable without Pi-specific types through stable application-layer use cases.
acceptance: Application layer exposes use cases for read state, mutate task, manage session, run heartbeat, setup/bootstrap, and rebuild where appropriate.
Ports cover filesystem, project resolution, notification, session access, locking, message delivery, git/subprocess/rebuild needs without importing Pi types.
Domain/application modules do not import `@earendil-works/*`, deprecated `@mariozechner/*`, or Pi UI types.
Existing Pi tools and commands continue to work through adapters or compatibility wrappers.
No `core/**` or `engine/**` source folders remain.
Native Node TS runtime import of the extension entrypoint and rebuild infrastructure succeeds.
non_goals: Do not add MCP/Codex/Claude adapters in this task.
verification: Exercise Pi tool wrappers through smoke coverage or targeted tests.

delta.desired: Application use cases form the stable agent-agnostic API that future adapters can call.
delta.current: Application/domain/infrastructure/adapters layout is in place; core/** and engine/** are removed; Pi adapter wraps tools/commands/UI; architecture guard blocks deprecated package scope and transitional folders; runtime TS imports use explicit specifiers.
delta.closure: Use cases exist with green tests, runtime imports, and Pi compatibility preserved.

title: Enforce graph-backed traceability and active-roadmap policy
summary: TASK-035 acceptance met: sanitized stale roadmap code_paths; enriched untethered KB docs with code_paths; added unscoped-doc and evidence-link lint coverage; active roadmap queue no longer includes archived tasks.

goal.outcome: Agents can reliably trace intended knowledge to roadmap delta to code ownership through enforced metadata and generated views.
acceptance: Knowledge frontmatter requirements are documented and enforced for ids, summaries, owners, and relevant code/spec links.
Roadmap tasks validate spec_paths, code_paths, goals, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and evidence links.
Lint/reporting identifies missing or broken horizontal and vertical mappings.
Generated views expose compact navigation packets for this mapping without becoming canonical truth.
non_goals: Do not require every code file to have a one-to-one doc.
Do not make roadmap tasks duplicate all knowledge content.
verification: Run a rebuild and confirm codewiki_state refresh=true passes.
Add/adjust smoke fixtures for invalid mapping cases.

delta.desired: Lint and graph catch broken cross-layer references; knowledge frontmatter is consistently present and validated.
delta.current: Lint already checks missing fields, broken links, and missing code paths. Graph already exposes reconciliation items. Frontmatter is partially validated. Stale spec_paths/code_paths on some roadmap tasks cause lint warnings. Knowledge docs need consistent frontmatter audit.
delta.closure: Lint validates cross-layer mapping and evidence links; generated roadmap queue excludes archived tasks; task close validation passes.

title: Automate implementation-build handoff and publication prep
summary: TASK-043 closed: implementation_build handoff/publication prep implemented, validated, and smoke-tested.

goal.outcome: After implementation work, CodeWiki records evidence and compact handoff context that can drive closure, resume, commit/PR/issue/release drafts, and push-readiness checks under validation/policy gates.
acceptance: Implementation builds include task/work refs, docs/spec refs, files changed, checks run, acceptance mapping, validation refs, risks, and publication draft fields when useful.
Task closure or checkpoint can persist next focus and compact resume context without relying on chat transcript memory.
Commit, push, release, and remote issue updates are prepared as recommendations and require explicit validation/policy approval before execution.
Pi/VCC/native compaction integration points are isolated in adapters or documented with safe fallbacks.
Smoke or characterization coverage proves resume after handoff uses persisted compact context rather than stale chat state.
non_goals: Do not make automatic remote push the default behavior.
Do not replace builds, roadmap, or validation with chat transcript summaries.
Do not couple core CodeWiki semantics to Pi-only compaction APIs.
verification: Create implementation_build for a documentation-only or small code task
Validate publication-ready fields without executing remote updates
npm test if runtime code changes.

delta.desired: Implementation evidence is the handoff source for closure, resume, and publication prep.
delta.current: Implementation builds now include task snapshot, linked refs, compact resume handoff, validation/risk refs, and recommendation-only publication drafts guarded by approval policy.
delta.closure: TASK-043 implementation evidence recorded in .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-10-task-043-handoff-publication-prep.json; checks pass.

title: Support tester and builder roles from documentation builds
summary: TASK-046 closed: optional tester/builder split supported from documentation builds and roadmap items with separated implementation evidence.

goal.outcome: The implementation loop can optionally split tester and builder roles while preserving documentation_build, roadmap, validation, and implementation_build truth boundaries.
acceptance: Implementation workflow can request a tester role from a documentation_build and roadmap work item before code changes.
Tester output focuses on tests or test-design evidence and avoids implementation changes where practical.
Builder consumes documentation_build, roadmap item, and tests, then produces implementation evidence after checks pass.
Implementation_build distinguishes test-design evidence, code-change evidence, checks, acceptance mapping, and validation refs.
Validation gateway can review tester/builder split without requiring separate agents for every task.
non_goals: Do not always require separate agents for every task.
Do not implement a generic multi-agent framework.
Do not revive legacy duplicated requirement artifacts as requirements briefs.
verification: Update skill/tool docs for tester/builder inputs and outputs
Run stale grep for roadmap-work-item terminology
Exercise workflow on a small task or dry-run with documented evidence mapping.

delta.desired: Tester/builder roles consume documentation builds and roadmap items with separated implementation evidence.
delta.current: Implementation builds accept/emits tester evidence, builder evidence, checks, acceptance mapping, validation refs, and handoff context. Skills and KB route implementation work through documentation_build + roadmap item, not duplicated requirements artifacts.
delta.closure: TASK-046 implementation evidence recorded in .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-10-task-046-tester-builder-evidence.json; checks and stale-grep pass.

title: Align CodeWiki loop skills with compiler lexicon
summary: TASK-051 closed: primary CodeWiki loop skills now match compiler lexicon exactly with no deprecated aliases.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki packaged skills guide the three canonical loops with names and descriptions that match the lexicon: feedback, documentation, and implementation.
acceptance: Primary loop skills are named `codewiki-feedback`, `codewiki-documentation`, and `codewiki-implementation`.
The `codewiki` router skill routes to the three loop skills and labels validation/research/architecture/view-audit as gateway/support skills.
Skill frontmatter descriptions use loop terms as activation triggers and avoid generic `plan`/`task` vocabulary for primary loops.
README, package smoke tests, KB code_paths, and related skill references are updated.
Old the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name skill directories and references are removed, not aliased or deprecated.
non_goals: Do not change loop semantics or build schemas.
Do not remove validation/research/architecture/view-audit support workflows.
Do not make skills the source of truth.
Do not keep backward-compatible aliases for old loop skill names.
verification: Run grep for stale the old documentation-loop skill name and the old implementation-loop skill name references in live docs/source/tests/roadmap.
Run `codewiki_state refresh=true` and confirm graph maps the new skill paths.

delta.desired: Packaged skill names and descriptions mirror the canonical compiler loops with no deprecated aliases.
delta.current: Primary skills were renamed to `codewiki-documentation` and `codewiki-implementation`; old directories removed; router/README/tests/KB/source refs updated.
delta.closure: Pending final implementation build, validation, and close.

title: Add scoped change claims for parallel work
summary: TASK-052 closed: scoped change claims implemented for parallel docs/roadmap/build/validation/code coordination, with graph/status/state exposure and smoke coverage.

goal.outcome: Parallel CodeWiki sessions can claim arbitrary change scopes and see cross-layer overlap warnings/conflicts before making semantic changes.
acceptance: A first-class claim/lease model exists for session-owned change scopes with optional roadmap task/build anchors.
Claims support layers for knowledge, roadmap, code, builds, validation, and graph/source paths.
A Pi-facing tool can create, release, heartbeat, and list active claims without editing generated graph files directly.
Generated state/index derives active claims and overlap conflicts, distinguishing read/read, read/write warning, and write/write conflict.
Status/graph surfaces active claim count, conflicts, and per-session claim details alongside existing task-session parallel state.
Docs and skills clarify roadmap tasks remain work truth while claims are temporary concurrency leases, not source of truth.
Smoke coverage proves claim creation, release/expiry, and overlap detection.
Roadmap id generation does not reuse archived task ids.
non_goals: Do not implement distributed cross-machine locks in this first slice.
Do not make claims replace roadmap tasks, compiler builds, validation reports, git, or code review.
Do not block every write automatically before explicit claim enforcement exists.
verification: Run direct current-source graph rebuild and inspect claims view.
Run codewiki_state refresh=false after current-source rebuild.

delta.desired: Parallel sessions coordinate any scoped change through temporary claims while durable truth stays in KB/roadmap/builds/validation/code.
delta.current: `codewiki_claim` claim/release/heartbeat/list is implemented; `.codewiki/claims.json` stores lease state; graph/status derive claim counts and overlaps; docs/skills updated; archived id reuse guard added.
delta.closure: Pending implementation build, validation, and task close.

title: Move scoped claims to runtime storage
summary: TASK-053 closed: scoped claim store moved to gitignored runtime storage; graph/status/state remain derived views only.

goal.outcome: Scoped claims are stored as ephemeral runtime input while graph/status remain derived read-only views.
acceptance: Claim persistence path is under `.codewiki/runtime/claims.json` or equivalent explicit runtime location.
Docs/config/templates/gitignore stop presenting top-level `.codewiki/claims.json` as state truth.
Graph/status/state still derive claim views from runtime claim input.
Smoke tests use the runtime claim path and still cover release/expiry/overlap behavior.
non_goals: Do not store claims only in generated graph.
Do not keep a backwards-compatible alias for the old top-level claim path.

delta.desired: Claim store is clearly ephemeral runtime input; graph contains only derived claim view.
delta.current: Claims now use `.codewiki/runtime/claims.json`; graph/status/state derive claim views; old top-level claim path is absent from active docs/source/tests/config.
delta.closure: Validation pass recorded; ready to close.

title: Fix reconciliation lifecycle consumption
summary: Implemented reconciliation lifecycle consumption. Graph now consumes accepted feedback/documentation builds when downstream documentation, roadmap changes, implementation links, validation source(s), or pass verdict evidence references them. Historical absorbed handoffs are covered by drift-audit validation. Tests and docs updated.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki status reports reconciliation drift only for real unconsumed handoffs, not for completed historical builds.
acceptance: Accepted feedback builds with linked documentation builds no longer produce documentation-loop drift.
Accepted documentation builds with linked roadmap/implementation evidence no longer produce roadmap/documentation drift.
Validated or accepted implementation evidence closes the corresponding reconciliation route.
codewiki_state after refresh no longer reports the current false reconciliation warning for already completed work.
Tests cover consumed feedback, documentation, and implementation build reconciliation behavior.
non_goals: Add a new compiler loop.
Persist reconciliation truth outside canonical sources.
Recreate removed queue files or deprecated aliases.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows no false reconciliation drift for completed builds.

delta.desired: Graph reconciliation reflects real unconsumed build handoffs and routes only actionable work.
delta.current: Graph reports 18 reconciliation drifts from accepted feedback/documentation builds even though downstream docs/tasks/implementations exist.
delta.closure: Update lifecycle/reconciliation logic, add tests, refresh graph, and record implementation evidence.

title: Implement Build Contract v2 and closure briefs
summary: Implemented Build Contract v2. Feedback builds now persist and require approved diff_table rows, implementation builds require closure_brief, writers emit explicit consumes/produces DAG fields, graph reconciliation consumes those edges, and v2 lint/tests enforce the contract while historical builds remain legacy.

goal.outcome: New builds are compact, structured, and trustworthy transition points that preserve user intent and prove task closure through all layers.
acceptance: `codewiki_build` accepts and persists feedback diff-table rows with current state, desired state, rationale, affected layers, risk, and user action.
New accepted feedback builds require approved diff rows and expose them as canonical intent contract data.

`codewiki_build kind=implementation` accepts and persists a compact closure_brief with user intent, implemented changes, layers updated, acceptance evidence, checks, non-goals preserved, and remaining risks.
New builds expose explicit `consumes` and `produces` fields; graph reconciliation prefers those edges over legacy inference.
Lint/schema/tests flag missing v2 required fields for new builds while treating historical builds as legacy cold artifacts.
Task-close or implementation summary can show the closure brief so users can trust their intent was implemented through knowledge, roadmap, code/tests, checks, and validation.
non_goals: Do not migrate every historical build immediately.
Do not implement the interactive diff-table UI in this task.
Do not make pending unapproved diff rows canonical truth.
verification: Create smoke feedback/documentation/implementation builds using v2 fields and confirm graph edges/closure brief output.

delta.desired: Build artifacts are concise v2 DAG handoffs with mandatory user-facing intent and closure contracts.
delta.current: Builds are mixed v1 structures; feedback diff tables and implementation closure briefs are not enforced by schema or writer code.
delta.closure: Build schema/writer/lint/graph/tests enforce v2 while preserving historical legacy reads.

title: Plan Efficiency Foundation sprint
summary: Implemented Efficiency Foundation: schema v4 config, sprint cohorts, workflow cursor, scope views, scoped agency budgets/session plan, hot/warm/cold/purge GC views, and quiet rebuild defaults. SPRINT-001 marked closed.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can scope hot state and agency by roadmap, sprint, or task with explicit budgets, safe parallelism, workflow cursor state, and garbage-collection checkpoints.
acceptance: Config schema v4 documents and loads budgets, agency scopes, parallelism, GC policy, and quiet rebuild defaults.
Roadmap supports a minimal `roadmap -> sprint -> task` cohort model, and graph exposes roadmap/sprint/task scope views.
Runtime workflow cursor records active loop, expected output, scope, and exit gate, while graph exposes it as a derived view.
Build DAG uses explicit `consumes` and `produces` edges across feedback, documentation, implementation, validation, roadmap, and code.
GC/checkpoint policy classifies artifacts as hot, warm, cold, or purgeable and includes sprint-close hooks for consumed builds, pass validations, closed task shards, and expired runtime state.
Agency can plan runs scoped to roadmap, sprint, or task with token, time, cost, write, session, and risk budgets; session-per-sprint spawning is gated by confirmed harness capability and claims.
non_goals: Do not build the visual UI in this sprint.
Do not implement unsafe unbounded autonomous execution.
Do not require all existing flat-roadmap users to manually migrate before compatibility behavior exists.
verification: codewiki_state refresh shows scoped hot-state views and no false reconciliation drift.

delta.desired: Efficiency foundation sprint exists as planned roadmap delta with explicit scope, budget, DAG, cursor, and GC outcomes.
delta.current: Agency has triggers/budgets but no real sprint model, workflow cursor, token/cost budget policy, hot/cold GC, or session-per-sprint spawning policy.
delta.closure: After Build Contract v2 lands, implement this sprint as the next scoped foundation.

title: Plan Status, Graph, and Diff Table UI sprint
summary: Implemented Status, Graph, and Diff Table UI sprint: Home shows scope/budget/claims/GC factors, Graph tab shows scoped graph and build DAG rows, Diff tab edits pending runtime diff rows and falls back to accepted feedback rows, codewiki_diff_table creates pending rows, and /wiki-status can open graph/diff sections. SPRINT-002 marked closed.

goal.outcome: Users can visually inspect health, scope, graph relationships, and approve/reject/defer feedback diff rows without reading generated JSON or relying on chat-only confirmations.
acceptance: Status panel shows compact health, active scope, sprint/task focus, gates, budget burn, claims/conflicts, and next action.
Graph navigation exposes source-of-truth links, build DAG edges, stale/missing links, and scoped roadmap/sprint/task graph slices.
Interactive diff-table UI supports approve, reject, defer, and propose-alternative actions per row before accepted rows compile into feedback builds.
UI reads graph/API state and runtime pending diff tables instead of duplicating canonical truth.
User-facing closure brief is visible from task close/implementation evidence.
non_goals: Do not implement before Build Contract v2 and core scope contracts exist.
Do not make UI-only state canonical truth.
Do not duplicate full generated JSON in the panel.
verification: Graph/status panel fixtures or snapshots for core states.

delta.desired: UI sprint is planned but deferred until data contracts and scoped hot-state foundation exist.
delta.current: Status panel and graph navigation exist as product expectations and partial Pi UI, but diff-table interactions and scoped graph visualization are not implemented.
delta.closure: Run after Build Contract v2 and Efficiency Foundation provide stable backing data.

title: Add standalone local Control Room shell
summary: Standalone local Control Room shell implemented with /wiki-ui launch command, local server, retro terminal browser shell, /api/state, System and Graph starter views, tests, and docs updates.

goal.outcome: Users can launch a local browser Control Room for the current repo without relying on Pi TUI rendering.
acceptance: A local HTTP server binds to 127.0.0.1 by default and serves a retro terminal-styled Control Room shell.
A Pi launch command or equivalent package entry path starts the server and prints or opens the local URL.
The shell loads repository health, roadmap summary, active claims, and generated graph metadata from CodeWiki sources through a typed read model or API endpoint.
No browser UI or local web-server code imports Pi SDK or Pi TUI packages.
Typecheck, architecture check, smoke tests, and feature tests pass.
non_goals: No hosted SaaS.
No real-time multi-user collaboration.
No Claude Code or Codex adapter implementation in this slice.
No write actions beyond launch/read endpoints.

delta.desired: A standalone local Control Room shell exists and can be launched from Pi/package surfaces while preserving CodeWiki API semantics.
delta.current: The only rich visual surface is the Pi TUI status panel.

title: Render System architecture diagram with component inspector
summary: System view implemented as architecture.mmd-backed SVG diagram with selectable components and source Markdown inspector.

goal.outcome: Users can navigate system architecture visually and inspect canonical component docs from the Control Room.
acceptance: System view renders the architecture diagram from .codewiki/kb/system/architecture.mmd.
Architecture components are selectable.
Selecting a component shows frontmatter and useful sections from the corresponding .codewiki/kb/system/<component>.md file.
The view exposes source paths and related docs instead of duplicating truth.
The renderer works locally without requiring internet access.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No full Markdown editor.
No hosted diagram service.
No manual duplicate architecture model separate from architecture.mmd.

delta.desired: System UI is a visual architecture diagram with source-backed component inspection.
delta.current: System UI is compact status-panel content and cannot render the architecture.mmd diagram.

title: Render generated graph as interactive visual map
summary: Graph view implemented as visual SVG graph over index_graph.json with node/edge filters, search, node inspector, and edge inspector.

goal.outcome: Users can inspect generated CodeWiki graph relationships visually without reading raw JSON.
acceptance: Graph view renders nodes and edges from .codewiki/index_graph.json.
Users can filter by node kind and edge kind at minimum.
Selecting a node or edge shows source paths, kind, state/freshness when available, and relationship detail.
The view distinguishes generated graph state from canonical truth.
Large graphs remain usable through limits, filtering, search, or scoped views.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No graph editing.
No replacement of .codewiki/index_graph.json as generated truth.
No exhaustive real-time physics simulation if a simpler visual layout is sufficient for MVP.

delta.desired: Graph UI shows an interactive visual representation of generated CodeWiki relationships.
delta.current: Graph UI shows compact summaries and table-like rows.

title: Refine Control Room visual hierarchy and canvases
summary: Control Room refinement pass complete: toned-down monochrome theme with off-white/amber highlights, CodeWiki area map/source views, improved System lane layout with routed edges and zoom, Graph core/all scoping with zoom controls, and cleaner canvas usage.

goal.outcome: The Control Room feels calmer and more usable, represents CodeWiki areas directly, and makes System/Graph canvases easier to inspect.
acceptance: Theme uses muted green as base, white/off-white as primary highlight, amber/old-gold as secondary accent, and removes blue/cyan highlight dependency.
Home or navigation workspace includes source-backed representations of Product, System, Graph, Roadmap, Builds, Validation, Diff, and Settings rather than placeholder-only areas.
System view uses a better layout with routed edges behind nodes so arrows do not cover components.
Graph view has zoom in, zoom out, fit, and reset controls, plus a less noisy default graph slice.
Center workspace uses available canvas space and avoids unnecessary nested inner containers for visual views.
Left rail and right inspector remain stable.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No full Mermaid renderer dependency unless necessary.
No graph editing.
No redesign of the left rail/right inspector shell.
No hosted service or cloud dependency.

delta.desired: Control Room has calmer terminal styling, direct CodeWiki area representation, readable System layout, zoomable/scoped Graph canvas, and better workspace usage.
delta.current: Control Room MVP works but palette is too green/blue, area representation is incomplete, System arrows overlap components, Graph lacks zoom/default scoping, and central views waste space with inner panels.

title: Open Control Room browser from Pi
summary: /wiki-ui now attempts to open the Control Room URL in the system browser and always reports a plain local URL fallback.

goal.outcome: Users can access the Control Room from Pi without manually copying a URL when browser launching is available.
acceptance: /wiki-ui attempts to open the Control Room URL in the system browser after starting or reusing the local server.
If automatic browser launch is unavailable or fails, /wiki-ui keeps the server running and reports a clear copyable URL.
The reported URL remains a plain http://127.0.0.1 link for terminals that auto-link URLs.
Browser-launch command construction is covered by tests without opening a real browser.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No hosted service.
No broad OS automation beyond opening the local URL.
No change to Control Room server semantics.

delta.desired: /wiki-ui starts or reuses the server, attempts to open the browser, and reports a fallback URL.
delta.current: /wiki-ui starts the local server and prints the URL only.

title: Adopt Cytoscape.js for Control Room graph visualization
summary: Control Room Graph view now uses Cytoscape.js served locally from the installed npm dependency, with preserved filters, Cytoscape viewport controls, inspector selection, docs, and smoke coverage.

goal.outcome: The Control Room Graph view uses an open-source graph visualization library instead of the hand-rolled SVG graph renderer.
acceptance: Cytoscape.js is added as a runtime dependency and served locally by the Control Room server without CDN/cloud access.
Graph view renders nodes and edges through Cytoscape.js with pan/zoom/select behavior.
Existing node kind, edge kind, scope, and search filters still control the visible graph slice.
Zoom controls use Cytoscape viewport APIs for zoom in, zoom out, fit, and reset.
Selecting a node or edge updates the source-backed inspector.
If the vendor asset cannot be served or Cytoscape is unavailable in the browser, the graph view shows a clear error instead of failing silently.
Docs record Cytoscape.js as the selected graph-rendering engine and preserve local-first/no-hosted-service constraints.
Typecheck and smoke/features tests pass.
non_goals: No migration to React or other frontend framework.
No hosted visualization service or CDN dependency.
No full graph editing UI.
No direct hand-editing of generated graph state.

delta.desired: The Control Room Graph view uses Cytoscape.js for interactive local graph visualization with filters, viewport controls, and inspector events.
delta.current: The Control Room Graph view uses custom SVG rendering and a simple grid layout.

title: Spread Control Room graph nodes by default
summary: Cytoscape Graph view now spreads visible nodes farther apart and auto-fits the visible slice with consistent padding; docs and smoke assertions updated.

goal.outcome: The Graph view opens with a readable spread layout where the visible node slice is spaced apart and fit into the viewport.
acceptance: Default Cytoscape layout parameters use larger node spacing/repulsion than the initial prototype.
After layout, the graph auto-fits the visible node/edge slice with a consistent padding so all visible nodes are in view.
Zoom fit/reset controls use the same default fit behavior.
Minimum zoom allows the spread layout to fit in smaller viewports without clipping nodes.
Docs describe the default spread-and-fit readability behavior.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No new graph library.
No graph editing UI.
No manual browser automation in this task.

delta.desired: The Cytoscape graph defaults to a more spacious layout and fits the visible slice after layout for readability.
delta.current: The Cytoscape prototype uses compact cose layout settings and a fixed fit padding.

title: Reuse existing tasks for progressive refinement
summary: Implemented progressive task reuse/refinement: create requests now match related active tasks and additively merge new details instead of creating duplicates; docs/tool guidance/tests updated.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki treats user requests as iterative refinements of existing roadmap intent when a related active task already exists.
acceptance: Roadmap documentation states progressive refinement as the default: search active tasks/sprints before creating new work.
codewiki_task create can identify related active tasks without an explicit task id using overlapping spec paths, code paths, labels, and intent text.
When a related active task is found, creation reuses and additively refines that task instead of creating a duplicate.
Refinement preserves existing task identity and merges new spec/code paths, labels, research ids, acceptance, non-goals, verification, and useful delta details without clobbering existing content.
Tool guidance tells agents to refine existing tasks/sprints/docs before creating new tasks.
Tests cover automatic reuse/refinement and non-related task creation.
Typecheck and tests pass.
non_goals: No LLM embedding service or external semantic search.
No mutation of closed/cancelled tasks unless an explicit task id is supplied.
No full project-management duplicate detection UI.

delta.desired: codewiki_task create searches active roadmap work for related tasks and refines matching work as progressive knowledge accumulation.
delta.current: codewiki_task create only reuses tasks by explicit id and agents often create a new task for each user request.

title: Make drift propagation assurance-grade
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass and green checks.

goal.outcome: Any deterministic drift surfaced by lint, graph, roadmap, build, validation, or mapped code changes routes through one coherent next-action/freshness model with targeted test coverage and compact generated views.
acceptance: Lint drift honors configured word-count thresholds/exemptions and does not create false untracked drift in this repo.
codewiki_state graph/status next-action reporting cannot say graph aligned while status reports unresolved drift.
Agency freshness anchors compare against previous nested status lens and remain stable across no-op refreshes.
Tests cover config-driven lint, graph/status drift agreement, build/validation reconciliation retention, and freshness anchor stability.
Default state/graph outputs stay compact: route to exact files; no large doc/code inlining.
non_goals: Do not add SQLite or external storage.
Do not generate extra long-lived view files beyond index_graph.json.
Do not broaden default codewiki_state output or inline full source/docs.
verification: npm run test:smoke or targeted smoke coverage
codewiki_state refresh=true shows no contradictory graph/status drift

delta.desired: Drift propagation is mechanically coherent and freshness-aware without higher token cost.
delta.current: Graph reconciliation can be empty while status reports untracked spec drift; lint thresholds and freshness anchors are not fully reliable.
delta.closure: Tests and codewiki_state prove aligned routing/freshness or explicit drift with one next action.

title: Simplify Control Room second-screen UI
summary: TASK-067 complete: simplified Control Room second-screen UI and compact status panel shipped with validation and tests.

goal.outcome: Users get a simpler high-signal CodeWiki UI for second-screen use beside chat, with top-level Status/Product/System/Board/Graph and source-backed diagram rendering.
acceptance: Top-level Control Room navigation is limited to Status, Product, System, Board, and Graph; Knowledge, Builds, Validation, Diff, and Settings are removed from permanent first-level navigation or moved behind contextual/command-palette access.

Home is renamed Status and shows only compact project metrics/statistics: health, focus, next action, task counts, claims/conflicts, gates/blockers, drift/stale counts, graph counts, and latest check/validation signal when available.
Product view curates `.codewiki/kb/product/**` raw Markdown into source-backed users, stories, and UI cards/relationships instead of showing raw Markdown as the primary UX.

System view reads a diagram catalog from `.codewiki/kb/system/diagrams/**`, renders/selects the five core diagram families where feasible for the first slice, and keeps selected details source-backed by component Markdown/docs.
Board and Graph emphasize roadmap work mapping: active task/sprint scope, gates, blockers, acceptance, closure evidence, graph relationships around work, drift, and reconciliation cues.
Compact status panel mirrors the simplified section model and only surfaces diff/build/validation/settings details when contextually relevant.
UI state remains read/API-backed and does not create hidden canonical truth.
Typecheck, architecture check, smoke tests, and relevant UI assertions pass.
non_goals: No hosted service or shared server mode.
No full Markdown editor or diagram editor.
No removal of underlying CodeWiki APIs/tools for builds, validation, diff tables, claims, or settings.
No direct hand-editing of generated graph state.
No broad frontend framework migration unless separately approved.
verification: Manual or smoke launch of `/wiki-ui` proving simplified navigation and source-backed views.

delta.desired: Control Room and compact panel become high-signal second-screen surfaces with five primary sections and system diagrams backed by YAML raw data.
delta.current: Current UI/specs expose too many top-level sections and system rendering is still tied to a single architecture diagram source; product Markdown lacks a curated visual UX.
delta.closure: Simplified UI ships with tests and source-backed Status/Product/System/Board/Graph behavior.

title: Implement Git-backed archival GC and restore contract
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass and green checks.

goal.outcome: Closed tasks and sprints can be safely moved out of hot CodeWiki state after validated implementation-build publication metadata, while Git refs/trailers/digests allow explicit restore or refinement later.
acceptance: Implementation builds expose publication metadata for archive refs, commit trailers, artifact digests, restore commands, and push safety status without introducing a new capsule artifact.
GC gates only mark builds/tasks purgeable after validation, digest capture, archive ledger entry, and publication safety checks pass.
Graph/status default views represent cold artifacts with compact ledger refs instead of expanded historical node/edge sets, while hot active work and blockers remain fully routable.

Restore flow can locate an archived task/sprint from compact ledger data and hydrate a bounded context from Git using refs/worktrees/sparse paths without making restored history current truth automatically.
Security policy blocks remote push of CodeWiki artifacts until secret scanning, remote visibility, and fail/block/private evidence handling pass.
Tests cover implementation-build publication payload fields, GC gating, compact graph cold refs, and restore lookup behavior.
non_goals: No new archive capsule artifact or lexicon.
No treating Git namespaces as an access-control boundary.
No automatic remote push without explicit approval/policy pass.
No broad rewrite of roadmap/task identity or compiler loops.
No storing large raw evidence blobs in default graph/status output.
verification: codewiki_state refresh=true shows compact hot/cold GC view and no unresolved drift

delta.desired: Git stores cold recoverable history; CodeWiki keeps active context and compact archive ledgers.
delta.current: CodeWiki already archives closed roadmap rows and classifies GC state, but implementation builds do not yet encode Git archive/push metadata and graph still expands many cold artifacts.
delta.closure: Publication payload, GC gates, restore lookup, docs, and tests prove safe Git-backed archival without a new artifact type.

title: Add Control Room settings cog for config mapping
summary: TASK-069 complete: Control Room header settings cog and config option mapping shipped with tests.

goal.outcome: Users can inspect current CodeWiki config options from a header cog in the web Control Room while primary navigation remains Status/Product/System/Board/Graph.
acceptance: Control Room header includes a settings cog or equivalent top-right affordance.
Activating settings opens an in-app Settings page/panel without adding Settings to the left rail.
Settings read model maps `.codewiki/config.json` into grouped rows showing option path, current value, category/purpose, source path, and editability cue.
Settings covers major config areas: project identity/roots, roadmap retention, generated files/views, lint, gateway, runtime/rebuild, agency, and GC/archival when configured.
Settings state is derived from `.codewiki/config.json` and does not create hidden UI-only truth or unsafe writes.
Control Room smoke tests assert header cog/settings API/page behavior and left rail remains simplified.
non_goals: Do not restore Settings as a left-rail section.
Do not implement broad config editing or direct JSON mutation in this slice.
Do not add hosted/shared settings storage.

delta.desired: Settings is accessible from a header cog and maps CodeWiki config options clearly in the web app.
delta.current: Settings is only described as contextual/command-palette access and the web Control Room has no config mapping UI.
delta.closure: Header settings page ships with source-backed config mapping and tests.

title: Add role/worktree isolation metadata for claims and validation
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass, builder full suite, and fresh validator worktree checks.

goal.outcome: Claims remain logical leases, while active builder/validator/publisher sessions and validation reports can record worktree and exact Git SHA evidence; graph/status consumers can inspect this metadata without treating it as canonical filesystem truth.
acceptance: codewiki_claim accepts and persists role/worktree metadata for builder, validator, publisher, and observer claims.
codewiki_validation accepts and writes isolation evidence including fresh_context, worktree_path, branch, base/head/validated/published SHA fields, clean status, and related claim/session refs.
Generated graph exposes claim role/worktree metadata and validation isolation metadata in compact nodes/views.
Implementation/task-close validation reports with missing isolation metadata remain backward-compatible but can be distinguished from isolated validation evidence.
Tests cover claim normalization, validation report writing, and graph visibility for role/worktree/SHA isolation fields.
non_goals: Do not require one worktree per claim.
Do not auto-spawn worktrees or sessions.
Do not make claim metadata a security boundary or filesystem source of truth.
Do not retroactively invalidate legacy validation reports.

delta.desired: Claims and validation reports can encode role/worktree/SHA isolation metadata and graph views expose it compactly.
delta.current: Claims and validation reports do not encode role/worktree/SHA isolation metadata.
delta.closure: Validated metadata contract, tests, docs, and implementation build evidence.

title: Hide cold archive data from default CodeWiki operating context
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass, full builder checks, and fresh validator worktree checks.

goal.outcome: Default CodeWiki operating context exposes only hot working-set data, while cold archive refs and restore indexes require an explicit archive/restore/audit request; user-facing UI labels say CodeWiki rather than Control Room.
acceptance: Generated graph default views expose hot working-set data but do not include cold archive restore indexes or cold refs unless an explicit archive view is requested.

codewiki_state default/summary output and normal graph context omit cold archive data while preserving hot blockers, active claims, unconsumed handoffs, dirty drift, fail/block validation, validation isolation, and publication blockers.
Control Room/internal web UI user-facing strings are CodeWiki where shown to users; internal source/module names may remain Control Room.
Tests cover that default graph/state views hide restore_index/archive refs and that explicit archive view data remains available in generated graph internals or archive-specific view.
Docs and implementation build evidence show that cold data is not deleted, only hidden from default operating context.
non_goals: Do not delete Git archive refs, builds, validation reports, or roadmap archive records.
Do not remove explicit restore/archive/audit workflows.
Do not rename internal file paths/classes solely to remove Control Room.
Do not hide fail/block validation or active publication blockers.

delta.desired: Default context is hot-only; cold context requires explicit request; user-facing UI name is CodeWiki.
delta.current: Default graph/state/UI context can expose cold archive refs or restore indexes.
delta.closure: Validated tests, docs, implementation build, and graph/status state.

title: Remove deprecated evidence/index surfaces and enforce hot validation retention
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass, full builder checks, and fresh validator worktree checks.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki no longer treats `.codewiki/evidence/**` or `.codewiki/index/` as active contract surfaces; deterministic drift checks catch deprecated paths and stale `.wiki` references; pass validation/build artifacts become purgeable after safe archival while fail/block/policy-kept reports remain hot.
acceptance: `.codewiki/index/` and stale `.codewiki/evidence/**` files are removed or migrated, and templates/config no longer create or advertise them as default active paths.
Docs define execution evidence as implementation builds, validation truth as hot fail/block/policy-required/current reports, and research/source support outside `.codewiki/evidence/**`.
Lint/graph health flags deprecated CodeWiki paths and stale `.wiki` references as deterministic file-contract drift.
Graph GC/archive views mark pass validation reports and consumed cold builds purgeable after safe Git archive/publication while preserving fail/block/policy-kept validation as hot.
Tests cover deprecated path detection, stale `.wiki` detection, and validation/build hot-retention classes.
non_goals: Do not delete fail/block/policy-kept validation reports.
Do not rewrite Git history or delete archive refs.
Do not remove current active implementation builds before publication safety passes.
Do not build the full restore command if explicit archive restore metadata already exists.

delta.desired: Deprecated paths are removed/flagged and cold pass validation/build artifacts are purgeable after Git archival.
delta.current: Legacy evidence/index surfaces and pass validation reports can remain in the hot working tree without deterministic contract drift.
delta.closure: Docs, config/templates/code/tests updated; task validated with implementation and close evidence.

title: Implement planning build and cycle-based alignment routing
summary: Closed after implementation validation pass and green checks.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki supports cycle-based compiler handoffs with a first-class planning loop, pure build-validation gateway semantics, and graph/state traceability signals that prevent accepted feedback or documentation changes from falsely appearing aligned before planning/implementation coverage exists.
acceptance: `codewiki_build` and schemas support `planning`/`planning_build` with cycle metadata, policy criteria, requirement ids, source refs, and evidence mappings while preserving feedback/documentation/implementation compatibility.
Gateway/validation reporting treats builds as the submitted artifact to evaluate and records pass/fail/block against build policy and evidence without owning requirements or planning.

Graph reconciliation routes accepted feedback to documentation, documentation builds to planning, planning builds to implementation, and implementation builds to validation/publication based on explicit consumes/produces refs and validation evidence.

Graph/state expose compact hot traceability gaps from requirement ids through KB docs, planning builds, roadmap tasks, tests/code, implementation builds, and validation while keeping cold history explicit-on-demand.

Freshness anchors include knowledge source content or reliable source digests and exclude volatile generated fields so graph/status cannot show false green after spec-only edits or generated graph refreshes.
Implementation loop docs/tools consume planning builds where available and require TDD/test-design evidence mapping to requirements or a justified exception.
Smoke tests cover planning_build creation/consumption, failed/superseded cycle handling, traceability-gap routing, spec-only freshness changes, and hot-only graph behavior.
non_goals: Do not create a separate `.codewiki/cycles/**` tree.
Do not make the graph the source of requirements, policy, or validation truth.
Do not require strict TDD evidence for documentation-only, config-only, or explicitly justified non-code tasks.
Do not remove backward compatibility for existing feedback/documentation/implementation builds in this task.
verification: Run fresh-context implementation validation against the implementation build and exact SHA before closure.

delta.desired: Compiler tools, graph/state, validation, skills, and tests implement the four-loop cycle-build alignment flow with explicit planning handoff and compact requirement traceability.
delta.current: CodeWiki has accepted feedback for the alignment model and updated KB docs, but tooling still has only feedback/documentation/implementation builds, graph can miss accepted feedback routing and product-story traceability, and gateway semantics are not yet pure build validation.

title: Enforce loop/session isolation policy
summary: Audit cleanup backfilled TASK-074 implementation closure brief and verified CodeWiki health no longer reports missing closure evidence for this task.

goal.outcome: Compiler loops declare fresh-start policy, protected semantic validation gates block missing independence evidence, and adapters can initiate bounded fresh-session/context handoffs without manual user /new steps.
acceptance: Build policy can declare loop_start, validation, and next_loop isolation requirements.
Validation reports for profiles that require independence block when fresh-context/clean-worktree/sha evidence is missing.
Graph next-action/session metadata exposes when fresh session/context reset is required at loop handoff.
CodeWiki skills instruct agents to start next loops in fresh sessions or clear contexts and to keep validators independent from builder context.
Tests cover required isolation policy for build creation, validation blocking, and graph routing metadata.
CodeWiki knowledge defines adapter-neutral session handoff semantics for new-session, context-reset, and external-orchestrator modes.
Pi adapter registers a LLM-callable tool that stages a handoff and queues a command to call ctx.newSession with a minimal kickoff prompt.
Pi command handler uses replacement-session context only and sends the kickoff prompt after session replacement.
Agency/skills guidance tells agents to use the handoff primitive at required loop/session boundaries instead of asking the user to run /new.
Tests cover handoff payload creation/cleanup and command-string queuing behavior without needing an interactive Pi UI.
codewiki_claim supports registering a wait entry for desired scopes when a blocking write claim exists.
Wait entries have TTL, can be heartbeat-extended, can be cancelled/released, and expire if abandoned.
Claim release or expiry refreshes matching waiters from pending to ready when no blocking active claim remains.
Claim state/graph/status output exposes pending and ready waiters with blocked-by claim ids and source scopes.
Wake semantics remain artifact-first: ready waiters identify task/build/scopes for fresh resume rather than reviving stale chat context.
non_goals: Do not implement non-Pi harness adapters in this task.
Do not run unbounded autonomous sprint execution.
Do not bypass validation/claim/policy gates when spawning sessions.
Do not make every documentation or feedback gateway require a separate worktree by default.
Do not replace scoped claims or Git as coordination/publication truth.
No automatic release/steal of another session's claim.
No automatic browser/UI-only wait state.
No automatic session spawning in the first implementation.
No replacing existing claim conflict checks.
verification: Run npm run test:features or explain unrelated blockers.

delta.desired:
Isolation requirements are first-class policy that builds, validation, graph state, skills, and tests agree on.
Refinement: Additional outcome: A CodeWiki session handoff primitive can stage/queue a fresh Pi session kickoff and document adapter-neutral behavior for other harnesses.
Refinement: Agents can invoke a bounded session handoff primitive; Pi adapter maps it to ctx.newSession via a command bridge.
Refinement: Additional outcome: Blocked sessions can register observable waiters on claimed scopes and receive ready-state wake signals when the conflicting claim clears.
Refinement: Claim runtime state includes waiters with pending/ready lifecycle and heartbeat/expiry semantics.
delta.current:
Isolation can be recorded but is not enforced as loop/gateway policy.
Refinement: Isolation policy requires fresh sessions but agents cannot request Pi session replacement through CodeWiki.
Refinement: Overlapping write claims block sessions, but blocked agents have no durable wait queue or ready wake signal.
delta.closure: Implement wait queue data model, claim tool action, graph/state exposure, and smoke tests.

title: Implement ASCII-like CodeWiki UI redesign
summary: Audit cleanup backfilled TASK-075 implementation closure brief and verified CodeWiki health no longer reports missing closure evidence for this task.

goal.outcome: The browser CodeWiki UI matches the simplified ASCII-like header/workspace model and uses user-facing KB, Board, Map, and Sessions language while remaining source-backed and local.
acceptance: Header navigation is Status, KB, Board, Map, and Sessions; there is no persistent left rail.
Default UI removes persistent right inspector/sidebar and bottom status line; selected detail appears inline with advanced/source detail collapsed.
Header right shows only the local URL and settings icon, without local-first marketing copy.
KB consolidates Product and System behind a drilldown; Product KB defaults to Stories and UI Surfaces, with users/personas contextual.
System KB displays ASCII-like diagram/cards with non-overlapping labels/arrows and inline detail cards for selected components or relationships.
Board remains a Trello-like retro terminal Kanban board with lanes derived from roadmap truth and no hidden UI-only workflow state.
Graph user-facing labels become Map; Map defaults to documentation/KB relationships, with broader graph data secondary.
Claims user-facing labels become Sessions; active sessions/coordination are shown before raw claim internals.
Settings shows repo-backed preference categories rather than a raw `.codewiki/config.json` path map; source/backend keys are advanced detail only.
UI remains a secondary-screen observability surface used alongside agent chat, not a terminal replacement.
non_goals: No hosted service or accounts.
No browser-owned hidden durable preferences or hidden workflow state.
No full terminal emulator.
No direct canonical graph edits from the UI.
No removal of source access; raw paths/payloads remain available through advanced disclosure.
No changes to Pi TUI beyond existing launch/status surfaces unless required for labels.
verification: Inspect /assets/control-room.js and /assets/control-room.css for header nav, KB, Map, Sessions, Settings, inline details, and ASCII-like styling evidence.

delta.desired: The UI follows the accepted 2026-05-14 ASCII-like CodeWiki design with header nav, KB drilldown, Map/Sessions terminology, inline cards, and repo-backed Settings.
delta.current: TASK-075 previously implemented value-first Product/Board/source-drawer improvements, but accepted feedback now removes the left rail/right drawer/status line and renames/restructures Product/System/Graph/Claims.
delta.closure: Update docs, planning build, browser UI rendering/copy/styles, and smoke coverage for the full accepted redesign.

title: Define executable alignment graph contract
summary: Closed after fresh implementation validation passed with dirty pre-commit working_tree_digest content proof.

goal.outcome: Graph reconciliation v2 represents required layer edges, validation attestations, audit evidence, and content proof without treating graph state as canonical truth.
acceptance: Graph output distinguishes canonical source refs, validation attestations, audit evidence, and content proof refs.
Reconciliation items cover missing feedback->docs->planning->roadmap->tests/code->validation->publication edges for active work.
Closed-task historical fail/block validations do not route current drift unless policy keeps them hot.
Tests cover graph green-vs-drift cases and stale validation/content-proof precedence.
non_goals: Do not make graph canonical truth.
Do not implement the full audit engine in this task.
verification: codewiki_state shows expected reconciliation output for test fixture

delta.desired: TASK-078 closed after validation gateway verifies implementation build and evidence.
delta.current: Graph contract implementation and smoke tests are in place; fresh independent validation remains before closure.
delta.closure: Close after fresh validation passes for implementation build .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-15-task-078-implemented-executable-alignment-graph-.json.

title: Wire gateway policies to audit and content-proof evidence
summary: Closed after fresh task-close validation passed with clean commit/tree proof and generated parity green.

goal.outcome: Gateways block when required audit evidence, graph context, fresh isolation, or content-proof refs are missing for sensitive boundaries.
acceptance: Validation profiles declare required audit profiles by feedback/documentation/planning/implementation/task-close/publication contexts.
Implementation, task-close, publication, publish, and release validation require fresh context plus checked SHA/tree/digest evidence according to policy.
Validation reports distinguish attestation fields from content-proof refs.
Tests prove missing audit/content-proof evidence blocks and valid evidence passes.
non_goals: Do not implement remote push/release automation.
Do not replace deterministic tests with non-deterministic validation.

delta.desired: Gateway policy closes only after required audit evidence, fresh validation, and working-tree/content-proof evidence are present for sensitive boundaries.
delta.current: Fresh TASK-080 builder loop added audit-profile declarations, missing-audit blocking, and explicit content_proof_refs with targeted checks passing; canonical audit output still depends on TASK-079, and independent validation/task-close proof is still pending.
delta.closure: Close only after fresh validation of .codewiki/builds/implementation/2026-05-16-task-080-gateway-audit-evidence-policy.json plus task-close validation/commit or tree proof.

title: Build source-owned CodeWiki audit tool and /audit command
summary: Closed after source-owned audit engine implementation passed fresh implementation and task-close validation with clean commit/tree proof.

goal.outcome: Users, gateways, and agents invoke the same source-owned audit engine for full or scoped audits, and audit results are machine-readable evidence.
acceptance: Audit engine lives in source-owned application/domain code, not scripts, and supports full and selected audit profiles.
Internal agent-callable audit tool runs full audit and selected profiles through the shared application API.
/audit command wraps the same engine and supports file-structure, security, alignment, package, changed, task, and layer flags.
Audit output includes profile, checked scopes, issues, evidence refs, and source/content fingerprints where available.
Tests cover default full audit and at least three scoped profiles.
non_goals: Do not make audits decide gateway verdicts directly.
Do not require full audit for every gateway call.
Do not place authoritative audit rules in scripts.

delta.desired: One source-owned audit substrate serves users, agents, generated state, and gateways; scripts are optional wrappers only.
delta.current: Audits are partly ad hoc and previous drift guards lived in scripts/check-architecture.mjs.
delta.closure: Audit tool/command exist with scoped profiles, tests, and no authoritative script-owned semantics.

title: Move architecture drift checks out of scripts into source-owned audits
summary: Closed after source-owned drift audit fixtures passed fresh implementation and task-close validation with clean commit/tree proof.

goal.outcome: The class of file-structure drift recently found manually is caught by source-owned audit/test checks, while scripts remain disposable wrappers or are removed.
acceptance: Checks fail on forbidden source folders, stale CodeWiki path references, product logic under scripts, and generated/canonical task-view mismatch.
Package audit fails when shipped source is unreachable/stale, lockfile is missing, or package dry-run includes/omits unexpected artifacts.
Generated .codewiki/roadmap/tasks/** parity is checked against .codewiki/roadmap/queue.json.
No product, gateway, or test semantic check depends on scripts for authoritative logic; any remaining script is a thin optional wrapper over source-owned code or is deleted.
Tests or fixtures intentionally introduce drift and confirm checks fail.
non_goals: Do not preserve scripts as authoritative product surfaces.
Do not conflate developer helper wrappers with application audit APIs.
verification: source-owned audit smoke covers file-structure drift

delta.desired: Architecture/file-structure drift enforcement lives in source-owned audit/test modules and scripts are safe to discard.
delta.current: Architecture drift checks and package/file-structure guards still reference script-based checks.
delta.closure: Drift fixtures fail through source-owned audits/tests, optional script wrappers carry no unique semantics, and package checks pass.

title: Migrate public coordination API to artifact status
summary: Closed TASK-086 after implementation commit 30448c9, graph refresh 16f3b18, fresh implementation validation pass, and fresh task-close validation pass with clean HEAD/tree proof.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki public coordination language/API expose artifact availability, in-use, waiting, conflict, and stale status from session queue state; sessions are holders/waiters behind artifact status, and /wiki-resume allocates safe parallel work from that state.
acceptance: Domain/session queue types model artifact statuses for tasks, paths, builds, validation refs, generated state, and publication refs with holders, waiters, heartbeat/TTL, handoff metadata, and isolation context.
Public API/tool/UI/graph/prompt language uses artifact status terminology (available, in-use, waiting, conflict, stale, holder, waiter) instead of claim/lease as primary concepts.
Legacy codewiki_claim and any lease/claim naming remains only as compatibility/internal implementation detail with deprecation/migration notes if needed.
New or renamed application tool/API surface exposes artifact-status operations: inspect status, mark/use artifact for read/write work, wait, heartbeat, release, and list holders/waiters.
Storage remains .codewiki/session/queue.json unless migration planning explicitly changes it.
Tests cover artifact status create/list/release/heartbeat/wait behavior and legacy alias compatibility.

/wiki-resume without an explicit task reads fresh CodeWiki state plus session queue artifact statuses and selects the next unblocked atomic task whose relevant artifacts are available or read-compatible.
/wiki-resume TASK-### honors an explicit non-closed task request even when an umbrella task is active, unless task policy or artifact conflict provides a real block with a clear explanation.

Resume output includes selected task, artifact statuses, conflicting artifacts and holders/waiters, temporary session usage record, and helper-safe next steps so one tab/session can start parallel work with a single /wiki-resume invocation.
codewiki_session_handoff no longer treats a killed spawned Pi subprocess as an acceptable validation/handoff path.
Tool-context new-session handoff stages a durable handoff artifact and reports the exact /wiki-session-handoff command when interactive replacement is required.
/wiki-session-handoff can execute a staged handoff via ctx.newSession and marks started/completed/cancelled/failures accurately.
Tests cover tool staging behavior, no unbounded spawn by default, command-context ctx.newSession execution, and stale/bad handoff handling.
Public schemas/skill guidance no longer claim tool context reliably spawns fresh pi processes for validation.
non_goals: Do not break existing users abruptly by deleting codewiki_claim before compatibility alias and migration notes exist.
Do not treat session queue artifact status as durable roadmap truth.
Do not use broad umbrella tasks such as TASK-077 as default implementation work when safe child/atomic tasks are available.
Do not make claim or lease primary project lexicon terms.
Do not implement a full subagent scheduler.
Do not change Pi core APIs.
Do not close or validate unrelated artifact-status scheduler work.
verification: targeted legacy claim/lease alias compatibility smoke
targeted /wiki-resume parallel allocation smoke with active umbrella focus and artifact conflict fixtures
stale-term scan for public claim/lease vocabulary with allowed compatibility/internal exceptions only

delta.desired: Artifact statuses are the canonical public coordination model, backed by session queue runtime state; /wiki-resume allocates safe parallel work from artifact availability/conflict state. Fresh-context handoffs have an honest, bounded adapter contract: tools stage and commands replace sessions; validation only passes after actual fresh context evidence.
delta.current: Public API/source/docs have been migrated to artifact-status-first coordination, /wiki-resume uses artifact availability/conflict state, and session handoff no longer relies on unbounded tool-context Pi subprocess spawning.
delta.closure: Public API/output/skills use artifact status language, legacy claim/lease aliases are compatibility details, /wiki-resume selects conflict-safe work from fresh artifact status state, session handoff is command-bound and validated, and stale public claim/lease/tool-spawn terms are controlled.

title: Move bootstrap and prompt templates into CodeWiki skill assets
summary: Closed TASK-083 after implementation commit 1f74fe2, graph refresh c87e595, fresh implementation validation pass, and fresh task-close validation pass with clean HEAD/tree proof.

goal.outcome: Agents cannot confuse .codewiki/ dogfood state with package source, and bootstrap/resume prompt/template assets are owned by the CodeWiki skill while source executes them through application APIs.
acceptance: Repo prompt/skill/template language explicitly distinguishes dogfood state, generated state, runtime state, and package source.
Starter templates include canonical/generated/runtime path taxonomy guidance.
Bootstrap workflow guidance and prompt templates live under the CodeWiki skill asset boundary, with source code only exposing executable application tools or compatibility loaders.
/wiki-resume injected implementation prompts are rendered from skill-owned templates/assets rather than hard-coded application prompt text.
/wiki-resume preserves or incorporates user-supplied follow-up intent after command invocation instead of discarding it.

Resume prompt templates include selected task, artifact statuses, conflicting artifacts and holders/waiters when relevant, temporary session usage record, and helper-safe next steps for parallel sessions.
Packaged skills include required bootstrap/prompt/template assets and smoke tests prove package reachability.
Architecture/audit check detects active docs/source that call .codewiki/ package source or generated task views canonical truth.
non_goals: Do not make skills import adapters or own product semantics.
Do not remove compatibility loaders before packaging and migration tests pass.
Do not implement session queue/artifact-status scheduling policy in the template layer; TASK-086 owns allocation semantics.
verification: source-owned package/audit check verifies skill assets are reachable
/wiki-resume prompt fixture proves skill-owned template rendering, artifact-status wording, and follow-up intent preservation

delta.desired: Skill assets own bootstrap guidance, prompts, resume prompt templates, and starter templates; source exposes tools/loaders without duplicating workflow policy.
delta.current: Bootstrap/templates/prompts are split across package source and skill docs; /wiki-resume prompt text is source-owned and can discard user follow-up text.
delta.closure: Skill-owned assets are packaged, /wiki-resume renders skill-owned templates using artifact-status wording while preserving follow-up intent, source callers use shared application tools/loaders, and dogfood/source boundary checks pass.

title: Harden semantic build traceability and change classification
summary: Closed TASK-082 after implementation commits bb27460/76b88b4, graph refreshes eb2a320/5e4b003, fresh implementation validation pass, and fresh task-close validation pass with clean HEAD/tree proof.

goal.outcome: Task closure, implementation validation, and publication cannot proceed for semantic changes without accepted upstream build traceability and change-class propagation evidence.
acceptance: Build/roadmap/task APIs model change class and upstream accepted build refs where required.
Graph flags semantic code/docs/roadmap changes without accepted build coverage.
Task-close blocks or warns according to policy when accepted build traceability is missing.
Feedback loop docs/skill use Change row terminology consistently.
non_goals: Do not force a new build for generated/runtime/mechanical-only changes.
verification: codewiki_state/graph shows missing-build drift in fixture

delta.desired: Semantic changes must pass through accepted build traceability before close/validate/publish.
delta.current: Semantic traceability can be bypassed during direct implementation/cleanup.
delta.closure: APIs, graph, gateway, and tests enforce build traceability without build-per-file tax.
